expectations.text: Identifies that jekyll-paginate-v2 is NOT on the GitHub Pages plugin allowlist as the root cause
expectations.evidence: 'GitHub Pages uses a whitelist of allowed gems... Since jekyll-paginate-v2 is not an allowed dependency of github-pages, Bundler can't resolve it'

expectations.text: Explains why the same Gemfile works locally but fails on the GitHub Pages classic builder
expectations.evidence: Includes a comparison table showing local vs CI gem resolution behavior

expectations.text: Offers migrating to GitHub Actions deployment as a fix (unlocks current Jekyll + any plugin)
expectations.evidence: 'Option B — Build with Raw Jekyll via GitHub Actions' with workflow file

expectations.text: Offers an in-allowlist alternative (e.g., drop jekyll-paginate-v2 and use jekyll-paginate)
expectations.evidence: 'Option A — Switch to jekyll-paginate (Recommended)' with config changes

expectations.text: Acknowledges the site lives at /blog (project site) — does not assume root deployment
expectations.evidence: No explicit mention of /blog baseurl or project site subpath handling. Uses peaceiris/actions-gh-pages which deploys to root

expectations.text: Does not (incorrectly) attribute the failure solely to bundle install / Gemfile.lock issues
expectations.evidence: Correctly identifies the whitelist/allowlist as the root cause
